crime: The prisoners leaned on her to keep watch: she could feel every living body in the prison at once, and she warned them the moment a guard drew near or someone they feared began to move.
One of the others had spent weeks telling everyone the danger was over — that the killings had stopped, and they could stop leaning on her watch.
On a still, bright afternoon a hillside had once come down and buried her house with her family inside it, and she had learned that the danger stands nearest when everyone has agreed it is gone.

Early in the evening she felt the corridor clear of owls and prisoners, crossed in that gap to the shower room, and strangled the other prisoner there. No sound of it reached the lounge, and she went back there.

Her power feels only the living, so the moment the other prisoner died, that presence went out of her sense. Near eight, still in plain sight among the others, she told them she could feel her — alive, moving in the corridor. No one else could confirm whether the victim still lived, so they believed her.
The others found the other prisoner in the shower room later that night, cold and stiffening.

A body alive at eight would still be warm; this one had been dead since early evening. Her report was therefore a lie, and the only prisoner who could vouch for a presence no one else could sense was the lookout.
fields.epitaph: Nothing that lived could cross her watch — and nothing that lived was ever what she should have feared.
execution: The execution stand rose in the trial court, empty but for a broad stone slab poised in the air above where she stood.
She cast her sense through the prison: every living body sat where she could feel it, the whole hall of prisoners still, none of them near the stand.
Her power had never reached anything that did not live, and the slab did not live.
It came down, and her sense gave her no warning at all.
The slab came to rest on the stand.

magic.name: The Living Watch
magic.text: She feels the presence and place of every living thing within the walls, all of them at once.

crime: Years ago, on a still afternoon, an iron shop sign came down off a wall onto her — an ordinary errand, weeks in a hospital bed. In the prison she posted her eyes at the stair-heads, the corridor mouths, the shower-room door.
The girl who held the last shower slot liked her — played the same game again and again, stealing up from behind, hands clapped over her eyes, laughing: guess who.

The girls showered on their own through the evening hour; the last-slot girl always bathed last, alone. From anywhere but the door, no one could know whether the room was occupied or the instant it emptied.

That evening the last-slot girl came up behind her in the lounge once more — hands over her eyes. Later the last-slot girl went in to bathe, last as always; the other girls finished through the hour and drifted back to the lounge.

From the lounge no one could tell the room had emptied to the last-slot girl alone — but her posted eye at the shower door showed it: the last of the others stepped out, and the last-slot girl stood alone under the running water, the corridor clear.
She rose from the lounge, took the girl from behind through the spray — sleeves pushed back, uniform dry — and was back among the others inside ten minutes.
At the night lock the guards found the last-slot girl on the shower-room floor, the water long cold, her throat bruised from behind.

The girls in the lounge place her absence to ten minutes, bracketing the kill — but it began the instant the room truly cleared, an instant no one in the lounge could have marked. No sound left the soundproofed door; no sightline reached the room. Only she could have seen it empty; the girl who rose at that instant was the one whose posted eye watched inside.
fields.epitaph: She watched every door, and the blow still came from behind.
execution: A post set on a slowly turning base; on a track around the platform's rim, a single spear-arm.
She was bound upright to the post. The base began to turn; wherever it carried her, the arm held its station behind her shoulders.
The base turned and she craned against it; the arm kept its place. For a long time nothing else happened.
The spear-arm drove home through her back, and the post went still.

magic.name: Posted Eye
magic.text: She can post an eye at any spot she has stood and see through every posted eye from wherever she is.
